Notes: FLY RIVER GIANT. Possibly the largest saltwater crocodile skull in Australia. Measurements verified by Roger Matthews. Some millimetres missing from rear of bottom jaw where it was butchered off; missing portion not added to skull total length. Skull total length (snout to back of mandible): 94.6 cm. Skull weight: 21.4 kg. ADDITIONAL MEASUREMENTS NOT IN STANDARD FIELDS: Snout/nose width 17.4 cm; Width across squamosal 'horns' (MCW): 21.9 cm; Mandible width: 6 cm; Width between bottom jaw hinges: 17.4 cm; Snout width horizontal at tip: 7.7 cm; Snout width vertical at tip: 5.8 cm; Height of skull: 30.3 cm; Mandible front teeth diameter: 2.9 cm; Top jaw main teeth diameter: 3.1 cm; Vertebrate (occipital) condyle ball: 4.3 x 5.1 cm; Back of horns to tip of nose: 76.1 cm. Live-animal TL unknown - skull-only specimen. Estimated live TL from Combined Ratio (5.4 multiplier): ~5.94 m. Skull is unusually slender (W:L ~51%) for a C. porosus this large - on the lower end of saltie range (typical 64-74%).
Source: Verified by Roger Matthews; provided by anonymous contributor (Australian collection)

Origin: Obo, Fly River, Papua New Guinea

Notes: Largest croc held alive in captivity. Hatched wild ~1900. Captured 1984 Finniss River, NT. Ascension 01/11/2024. Weight ~1000 kg. Guinness World Record holder. Skull missing approx 30 mm of tip; TL straight-line measurement does not include 15-25 cm of missing tail. Skull is skin-on (not dried/cleaned). ADJUSTED RATIO SCENARIOS (for fairer comparison to W&W literature, which uses dried skulls): Hypothesised full DCL 680 + MHW 460 + TL 570 → Combined Ratio 5.00 (matches Mary River). Hypothesised full DCL 680 + MHW 470 + TL 560 → Combined Ratio 4.87. 4% skull-shrinkage adjustment (skin-on to dried equivalent): DCL 652.8 + MHW 451.2 + TL 570 → Combined Ratio 5.16, HL:TL 8.73, W:L 69.1% (sits cleanly in big-specimen cluster: Edgar 5.49, Lolong/Old Charlie 5.37, Mary River 5.00).
Source: Australian collection official record card
